title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Lewis, 18, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her friend Lily, who died of brain cancer.
She is participating in the marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute alongside her father.
Lewis aims to raise money for cancer research, inspired by a personal loss and a commitment to support cancer patients.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes, 55, was sentenced to 8-10 years in prison for rape and child pornography charges.
Dos Santos Mendes, an undocumented immigrant, will be deported to Brazil after serving his prison sentence.
The charges included two counts of rape, one count of assault to rape, and possession of child pornography.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots release Stefon Diggs, creating $17 million in cap space for potential wide receiver acquisitions.
New England considers signing Alec Pierce or trading for A.J. Brown to fill WR1 position.
Draft options for Diggs' replacement include Texas A&M's KC Concepcion or Washington's Denzel Boston.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Boston Children's Hospital and their Miles for Miracles program.
Poehler's daughter, a liver transplant survivor, received life-saving care at Boston Children's Hospital at 11 months old.
The marathon honors the care her family received and aims to raise funds for research and treatments for other children.

title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Over 350 readers responded to a survey on U.S. strikes on Iran, with 54% supporting and 43% opposing.
Supporters cite Iran's regional influence and regime's actions; some Iranians believe pressure could weaken the government.
Opponents fear escalation into broader war and highlight lack of congressional approval and domestic priorities.

title: Paul Pierce tells Celtics fans to keep expectations in check over Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advises Celtics fans to temper expectations as Jayson Tatum nears return from Achilles injury.
With a 41-20 record, Boston Celtics await Tatum's contribution for a playoff push despite possible minutes restriction.
Tatum may not be the main scorer immediately, allowing younger players like Hugo Gonzalez to gain experience.

title: Patriots are reportedly releasing WR Stefon Diggs after 1 season in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots are releasing Stefon Diggs after one season, saving $16.8 million in salary cap space.
Diggs achieved over 1,000 receiving yards in 2025 but faced a high cap hit and legal issues.
New England now seeks a new top wide receiver after Diggs' release, aiming to strengthen their roster.

title: High court judge denies DiZoglio’s ask for outside attorney in audit lawsuit
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The SJC denied State Auditor Diana DiZoglio's request to hire an outside attorney for her audit lawsuit against legislative leaders.
Attorney General Andrea Campbell moved to strike DiZoglio's lawsuit, arguing that DiZoglio lacks authority and that her office controls legal disputes.
Judge Wendlandt ruled DiZoglio must respond to Campbell's motion by Thursday, dismissing DiZoglio's claims of a conflict of interest.

title: Former school bus driver indicted in Hyde Park crash that killed 5-year-old boy
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jean Charles, former Boston school bus driver, indicted for homicide in crash that killed 5-year-old Lens Joseph.
Charles, 39, allegedly drove away after missing stop, resulting in Lens Joseph's death on April 28, 2025.
Transdev's poor record-keeping and training practices scrutinized following Charles's expired bus certificate revelation.

title: Wedding photo company’s sudden bankruptcy leaves more than 100 Mass. couples scrambling
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Yours Truly Media, a California-based wedding photo company, filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy, affecting over 100 couples in Massachusetts.
The bankruptcy leaves couples scrambling for new photographers, with some losing deposits upwards of $3,000 that they can't currently recover.
Between May 2020 and March 2026, the Massachusetts Attorney General’s Office reported 10 consumer complaints against Yours Truly Media.
